axiom_metadata.remediation: Add break condition or modify loop to have a termination condition. Consider using 'while' instead of 'loop' when a termination condition exists.

rule_designs.detection_logic: tree-sitter AST: find 'loop_expression' or 'infinite_loop' nodes; traverse body for break_statement; flag if none found and no while condition
rule_designs.gate_notes: Meets complexity budget, tree-sitter approach appropriate

axiom_metadata.remediation: Add a bounds check before division: if divisor == 0 { handle_zero() } else { a / b }. Or use a.checked_div(b) which returns Option.

rule_designs.detection_logic: AST + constant folding: detect '/' or '%' binary_expression nodes; check denominator operand; flag if literal 0
rule_designs.gate_notes: Critical severity appropriate, simple AST detection sufficient

axiom_metadata.remediation: Replace mem::uninitialized() with MaybeUninit<T>::zeroed() or properly initialize the value. Use MaybeUninit<T> and call assume_init() only after all fields are initialized.

rule_designs.detection_logic: tree-sitter AST: detect call_expression with function name containing 'mem::uninitialized' or 'std::mem::uninitialized'
rule_designs.gate_notes: Critical severity appropriate, simple call detection

axiom_metadata.remediation: Replace with ? operator for propagation, unwrap_or(default), unwrap_or_else(|| ...), or proper match/if let handling.

rule_designs.detection_logic: tree-sitter AST: detect method_call_expression with 'unwrap' or 'expect'; check parent context for guard (if let, match, while); flag if unguarded
rule_designs.gate_notes: Critical severity appropriate, control flow analysis needed

axiom_metadata.remediation: Use .get(index) instead of [index] for Option-based handling, or add a bounds check: if index < arr.len() { arr[index] } else { handle_oob() }.

rule_designs.detection_logic: tree-sitter AST: detect index_expression nodes; flag if using direct [] indexing (not .get())
rule_designs.gate_notes: Simple detection, conservative approach with .get() recommendation

axiom_metadata.remediation: Remove the unreachable code, or add #[allow(dead_code)] if intentionally kept for documentation or future use.

rule_designs.detection_logic: control flow AST: detect return_statement or panic! macro_invocation; check for sibling statements after them that are unreachable
rule_designs.gate_notes: Minor severity, conservative detection of unreachable code

axiom_metadata.remediation: Use partial_cmp and handle Ordering::Less, Ordering::Greater, Ordering::Equal explicitly. Or use f32::is_nan() to check before comparison.

rule_designs.detection_logic: type-aware AST: detect binary_expression with comparison ops on f32/f64 types; flag == or != comparisons (NaN always returns false for these)
rule_designs.gate_notes: Focus on == and != which are the problematic NaN comparisons

axiom_metadata.remediation: Use checked_add(), checked_sub(), checked_mul() and handle the None case. Or use saturating_* for clamping behavior.

rule_designs.detection_logic: AST + constant folding: detect binary_expression with arithmetic ops on integer types; flag if not using checked_/saturating_/wrapping_ methods
rule_designs.gate_notes: Meets complexity budget, simple AST detection

axiom_metadata.remediation: Replace with ? operator for propagation, unwrap_or(default), unwrap_or_else(|| ...), or proper match/if let handling.

rule_designs.detection_logic: tree-sitter AST: detect method_call_expression with 'unwrap' or 'expect'; exclude test modules and guard contexts
rule_designs.gate_notes: Major severity, excludes test code which is appropriate

axiom_metadata.remediation: Use lazy_regex::lazy_regex! or regex::regex! for compile-time validation. Or validate pattern with Regex::new().is_ok() before using.

rule_designs.detection_logic: AST + constant analysis: detect call_expression with 'Regex::new' or 'regex::Regex::new'; flag if not using lazy_regex or regex! macro
rule_designs.gate_notes: Simple call detection, recommends safe alternatives

axiom_metadata.remediation: Verify loop bounds match intent: for i in 0..n gives 0..n-1; for i in 0..=n gives 0..n. Check if comparison operator should be < instead of <=.

rule_designs.detection_logic: AST pattern matching: detect for_expression with range including .len() or .iter().count(); flag patterns like ..= with .len() that may cause off-by-one

axiom_metadata.remediation: Remove the explicit drop() call or ensure the variable is not used after scope exit. Use std::mem::forget if drop prevention is intentional.

rule_designs.detection_logic: AST + scope analysis: detect explicit drop() call_expression or macro_invocation; flag as potential double drop
rule_designs.gate_notes: Simple detection, warns on explicit drop

axiom_metadata.remediation: Add explicit parentheses to clarify intended grouping: (a || b) && c instead of a || b && c.

rule_designs.detection_logic: AST pattern: detect binary_expression containing both && and || without outer parentheses; flag as potentially confusing
rule_designs.gate_notes: Minor severity, readability concern rather than correctness

axiom_metadata.remediation: Fix the contradictory condition logic. Likely a copy-paste error or incorrect operator.

rule_designs.detection_logic: AST + range analysis: detect binary_expression with comparisons on same variable with mutually exclusive ranges; flag always-false conditions
rule_designs.gate_notes: Minor severity, needs careful range analysis to avoid FPs

axiom_metadata.remediation: Remove the redundant assignment if the first value is never used. Or initialize the variable with the correct value directly.

rule_designs.detection_logic: dataflow AST: track let_declaration and assignment_expression; detect when variable is assigned without reading previous value
rule_designs.gate_notes: Highest complexity due to dataflow, conservative approach
